_raw_content: Your body decided you were unsafe long before your mind made a story about it. Every pattern you run — the people-pleasing, the shutdown, the constant scanning — is your nervous system honoring an old agreement. You don't need to heal the story. You need to release the body from the contract it signed when you were too young to read the terms. Safety isn't built with affirmations. It's built in the marrow. One honest breath at a time.
slide_1_hook: You're not anxious — your body is still honoring an old contract.
slide_2: Before the story, before the label, before the diagnosis — there was a moment your nervous system decided: this world is not safe.
slide_3: You are allowed to release an agreement you never consciously made.
slide_4: Healing isn't rewriting the narrative. It's letting your tissues finally exhale the war they've been holding since before you had language for it.
slide_5: Safety is not a thought. It is a frequency your bones learn to hold. Slowly. Without proof. Without permission from the mind.
slide_6: I am releasing contracts that were never mine to carry. My body is learning a new agreement — one breath, one moment, one tremor at a time.
caption:
You were never broken.

You were bound.

Bound by an agreement your nervous system made in a moment of overwhelm — before you had words, before you had choice, before you had any way to say no.

And every survival pattern you've been running since then has been your body keeping that contract alive. Loyal to the wound. Faithful to the emergency.

But the emergency ended.

Your body just never got the message.

This is the real work. Not mindset. Not mantras. The slow, sacred act of letting your nervous system update its terms.

You don't force it. You don't rush it. You breathe into the places that forgot they were allowed to soften.

And one day — not with a bang but with a breath — the contract dissolves.

And you feel your own body choose you for the first time.
